settings.editor:
Learning & Development Lab Director

Mission:

The mission of the Learning & Development (L&D) Lab Director is to coordinate the research of and distribution of information related to learning and growth trends in the AEC industry. This information which may be related to trends including AI, hiring trends, PM practices, etc. may be distributed through content channels (i.e. podcasts, articles, and videos) and client services (mastermind groups, learning sessions, forums, etc.).

Outcomes:

  1. Oversee EMI's AEC PM Certification process which is the creation and management of a new industry wide certification. The certification is tied to our PM development programs administered by our Learning & Development team, but the L&D Lab Driector will oversee the certification process including the PM AssistTM service which is a service focused on reinforcing PM tactics to AEC project managers that have previously gone through EMI's learning and development programs.

    The PM Assist service consists of quarterly live webinars on trending PM tactics, and the delivery of short micro-learning videos.
    • Source topics and speakers for quarterly live PM Assist webinars for EMI's PM community. Topics may be similar to those listed in Item #1.
    • Facilitate each of the quarterly PM Assist webinars.
    • Deliver one PM Assist webinar per year as the featured speaker. This session may be focused on topics related to but not directly PM topics (i.e. AI in project management).
  2. Create a membership-based revenue stream with a minimum annual revenue of $150,000.
    • Create and run a mastermind group and/or forum with a recurring fee.
    • Create content for membership based on industry trends (AI, hiring trends, PM practices, etc.)
    • Host periodic interviews or fireside chats with industry professionals for membership
  3. Host one of EMI's existing podcasts, or create and host a new one
    • Host one of AEC's two existing podcasts, The AEC Engineering & Technology Podcast or The Engineering Project Management Podcast, or create and host a new one. EMI's content team will handle all of the technical and production work.
    • Host at least two episodes per month.
    • Provide EMI's content team with topics/guests for the podcast so they can reach out to the guests and coordinate the podcast.
  4. Oversee the publication of EMI's annual Future of Work (FOW) Trends Report - a report that EMI publishes each year with a partnering organization and a third party who assists in the survey and report preparation.
    1. Manage the FOW production project from EMI's side
    2. Attend bi-weekly meetings during the report preparation period (December through April)
    3. Review documentation from the third party vendor and provide feedback and direction
    4. Present the report findings through webinars, live speaking engagements and content channels (typically most of these presentations are virtual)
    5. Work closely with marketing on tracking report downloads and traction

Qualifications

  • A bachelor's degree from an accredited college or university (engineering, architecture, land surveying or related field is a plus but not required).
  • Minimum of five (5) years of relevant experience in the AEC industry (project management experience is a plus).
  • Excellent communication and speaking skills are a MUST.

Benefits

  • Work Location: Remote with 3 to 5 trips per year to Headquarters (Midland Park, NJ) plus travel to conferences if you are interested.
  • Full -Time Hours: The position is full-time and requires 35 hours per week, but EMI is very flexible as to the time of day your work is conducted.
  • Retirement Benefits: EMI offers a 401k retirement plan to all full time employees.
  • Profit Sharing: You will have access to a quarterly profit sharing program, so that as EMI grows, all team members are rewarded monetarily for their contributions to EMI's growth.
  • Access to high level leaders: Through conducting interviews for content development, you will have access to and converse with the highest level of leaders in the architecture, engineering, and construction industries.

Job Type

Full-time

Expected Salary

$80,000 - $105,000 per year* Salary range may vary based on applicants experience level and could be higher than expected.

Work Location

Remote with 3 to 5 trips per year to NJ Headquarters

What Makes the Engineering Management Institute a Great Place to Work…

The Engineering Management Institute is the gold standard learning and development resource for engineers and technical professionals worldwide. EMI empowers technical professionals to become the leaders they need to be to solve the world's biggest problems by providing highly relevant content and training that transfers back to their jobs.

EMI is a great place to work as we work with top engineering organizations globally, we are growing fast, and focus greatly on helping our team members achieve their personal and professional goals. We do this by following our guiding principles (give, guide, and grow).  We ask all EMI team members to give their all, guide each other, and grow together.  We also employ a quarterly profit sharing program, so that as EMI grows, all team members are rewarded monetarily for their contributions to EMI's growth.

If you are interested in joining a dynamic team, and working on exciting growth-oriented projects, please consider this role. Joining the EMI team may be an avenue to fast-track the achievement of your professional and personal goals.

Apply Today by Completing this Form
